note: Clientside Validation is a form-alter that decorates elements with data-rule-*/data-msg-* attributes and defines the CvValidator plugin type; the jQuery submodule supplies the actual engine. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: I enabled the clientside_validation module but forms are not being validated in the browser before submit. What else must I enable for validation to actually run?
evals.expected_output: The base module only writes data-rule-*/data-msg- attributes; enable the clientside_validation_jquery submodule, which loads the jQuery Validate engine that enforces them.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal form element with #required or a #pattern, how does clientside_validation turn Drupal validation into a client-side rule? What HTML attributes end up on the element?
evals.expected_output: A CvValidator plugin's getRules() output is written by CvValidatorBase::addValidation() as data-rule-<name> and data-msg-<name> attributes (translated messages) on the element, matched to the element #type or attribute.

evals.prompt: I want to add a brand-new client-side validation rule from my own Drupal module. Which plugin type do I implement and where does the class go?
evals.expected_output: Implement a CvValidator annotated plugin in src/Plugin/CvValidator/, extending CvValidatorBase, with a supports (types/attributes) definition; discovered by plugin.manager.clientside_validation.validators.

evals.prompt: How do I stop clientside_validation from adding client-side validation to one particular form or element?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_clientside_validation_should_validate($element, $form_state, $form_id) and return FALSE for the form/element to skip.

evals.prompt: Which validation rules does the base clientside_validation module ship out of the box (not counting the jQuery submodule)?
evals.expected_output: Base module ships required, email, url, url_internal_external, min, max, maxlength, and step validators. (pattern and equal_to come from the jQuery submodule.)

evals.prompt: How do I set a custom client-side error message for a required field or a pattern on a Drupal form element rendered by clientside_validation?
evals.expected_output: Set the element render key #required_error (or #pattern_error, etc.); the validator uses it, otherwise it builds a default from the element #title.

evals.prompt: Why do Cancel/Preview style submit buttons skip client-side validation with clientside_validation, and how is that implemented?
evals.expected_output: Submit buttons whose #limit_validation_errors is empty get the CSS class 'cancel', which jQuery Validate treats as a skip-validation button.
